comment.body:
mean reversion trading is about to catch underpriced side. 
market goes down to a certain extreme, you know that market is mean reverting, you chose a call that you meassures that it is underpriced, and you go for it, and boom you got convexity, fast profit, hit n  run.
this is mean reversion trading.
comment.raw_body:

this is mean reversion trading.

comment.body:
however, you have to know that mean reversion trading can be very very risky if you hit on a momentum.
all your profit can be lost, if you are sized up.
kelly fraction is very important here, and mean reversion trading is not the strategy you want to martingale into.

comment.body:
in such an environment, like what we see: intraday negative speed, lower downside vol higher downside gamma, net negative drift...
the best is i think to short otm calls and long put, just as i do: i have a long put, and selling intraday calls (i don't signal these, bcs i know many can't sell calls), mostly 22 deltas to catch vanna effect. 
and as a side trade, i go for intraday reversion trades

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
why? 
bcs OTM calls are decaying due to time, spot and vol as well intraday. 
puts are naturally printing as the "market wants down", but its a crowded trade, not necessary fast enough.
while intraday mean reversion calls print fast

comment.body:
with reversion trades (long calls), you have to be very precise on timing bcs there is a lot of theta decay. 
i like ITMs better here, simply bcs they are less crowded than the OTMs... and those upside calls or near-ATMs can be heavily sold on an upstick, an so they won't necessary print enough and fast

comment.body:
and here ATM put is fine, and then let it run if rejection manifests.
why? bcs upside vol move was a result of sudden call bid, plp jumped from calls to puts, and those ATM puts (and slightly OTMs) where eventually sold a bit relatively.
and if vol momentum doesn't occur, then that upstick will be faded, and you want to be in puts for a cheaper price
